revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:MediaWiki:Protect-text]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of MediaWiki:Protect-text]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>
#redirect[[en:MediaWiki:Protect-text]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of MediaWiki:Protect-text]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category New automatic wikipedia redirects
removing these dumb broken links that cause problems

revision.text:
{{fmbox |type=system |image=[[File:Full-protection-shackle-keyhole.svg|35px|alt=|link=WP:PP]] |text=
'''Important policies and items to note when applying or modifying page protection:'''
  • Your actions must comply with [[Wikipedia:Protection policy|Wikipedia's protection policy]] at all timesds.
  • The [[WP:30/500|extended confirmed]] protection level may be applied to a page only after semi-protection has been shown to be ineffective, or following authorization from the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee|Arbitration Committee]].
  • If you are lowering, shortening, or removing page protection that was applied by another administrator, you should review the protection logs for the page '''first''' and verify that there aren't any important details, discussions, links, or other information in the protection summary that might explain the reason behind the decision to apply protection, or the level or duration that was chosen.
  • If this page was protected '''as an [[Wikipedia:Office actions|office action]]''', you '''may not''' modify or remove the protection unless you have ''proper authorization from the Wikimedia Foundation Office''.
  • If you are modifying or removing page protection that was applied by another administrator because it was an action that you disagree with or believe to have been made in error – or if any level of opposition by the administrator is reasonably likely – '''you are expected to have properly communicated and discussed the matter with the applying administrator before proceeding'''.
}} {{Collapse top|title=Technical instructions and special cases|border2=0|bg=silver}} ;General information and instructions * By default, page protection is applied for an indefinite period of time unless you specify a time for it to expire. * To specify an expiration, you may either enter a length of time (e.g. "31 hours", "5 days" or "1 week"), or enter a specific date (e.g. "1 {{#switch:{{CURRENTMONTH}}|1=February|2=March|3=April|4=May|5=June|6=July|7=August|8=September|9=October|10=November|11=December|12=January}} {{#ifeq:{{CURRENTMONTH}}|12|{{#expr:{{CURRENTYEAR}}+1}}|{{CURRENTYEAR}} }}") using the [http://www.gnu.org/software/tar/manual/html_node/Date-input-formats.html GNU standard format]. * To remove protection currently applied to a page, modify the specific protection type (edit, move, creation) and set the protection level to "allow all users". Leave the settings unchanged on the protection types that you do not wish to modify. * Edit protection and move protection can be applied separately or together, and set to different protection levels and to expire after different lengths of timesds. ** If applying page protection to a completely unprotected page, the page protection form will automatically update the move protection settings to match the edit protection settings you enter, unless you specify different move protection settings. *** To specify move protection settings that are ''different'' than the edit protection settings you entered, tick the "unlock further protect options" checkbox and enter the move protection settings accordingly. *** To apply ''no'' move protection alongside edit protection, set the move protection level to "allow all users". * ''If you are not using an automated tool to modify or apply page protection, please update the [[Template:Protection templates|protection templates]] on the page after applying your changes.'' ;Important technical information * Avoid using non-integer values when entering an expiration (such as "2.37 weeks" or "1.84 days"). The MediaWiki software has interpreted non-integer expirations '''[[Wikipedia:Village stocks#SQL for the Time-Traveling Block Breaker award|very badly in the past]]''' before. * [[WP:PC1|Pending changes protection]] is unavailable for use on talk pages or anywhere within the user space. * Cascading protection is only available when applying full edit protection to a page. * The confirmed or autoconfirmed user right [[Special:ListGroupRights|is required]] in order for users to move ''any pages at all''; the page's protection status is completely irrelevant. ;Useful links * All pages that are currently protected from '''editing''' are publicly visible at [[Special:ProtectedPages]]. * All pages that are currently protected from '''creation (salted)''' are publicly visible at [[Special:ProtectedTitles]]. * All pages that are currently under '''pending changes protection''' are publicly visible at [[Special:StablePages]]. {{Collapse bottom}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: overwriting with templates included
revision.text:
{{fmbox |type=system |image=[[File:Full-protection-shackle-keyhole.svg|35px|alt=|link=WP:PP]] |text=
'''Important policies and items to note when applying or modifying page protection:'''
  • Your actions must comply with [[Wikipedia:Protection policy|Wikipedia's protection policy]] at all timesds.
  • The [[WP:30/500|extended confirmed]] protection level may be applied to a page only after semi-protection has been shown to be ineffective, or following authorization from the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee|Arbitration Committee]].
  • If you are lowering, shortening, or removing page protection that was applied by another administrator, you should review the protection logs for the page '''first''' and verify that there aren't any important details, discussions, links, or other information in the protection summary that might explain the reason behind the decision to apply protection, or the level or duration that was chosen.
  • If this page was protected '''as an [[Wikipedia:Office actions|office action]]''', you '''may not''' modify or remove the protection unless you have ''proper authorization from the Wikimedia Foundation Office''.
  • If you are modifying or removing page protection that was applied by another administrator because it was an action that you disagree with or believe to have been made in error – or if any level of opposition by the administrator is reasonably likely – '''you are expected to have properly communicated and discussed the matter with the applying administrator before proceeding'''.
}} {{Collapse top|title=Technical instructions and special cases|border2=0|bg=silver}} ;General information and instructions * By default, page protection is applied for an indefinite period of time unless you specify a time for it to expire. * To specify an expiration, you may either enter a length of time (e.g. "31 hours", "5 days" or "1 week"), or enter a specific date (e.g. "1 {{#switch:{{CURRENTMONTH}}|1=February|2=March|3=April|4=May|5=June|6=July|7=August|8=September|9=October|10=November|11=December|12=January}} {{#ifeq:{{CURRENTMONTH}}|12|{{#expr:{{CURRENTYEAR}}+1}}|{{CURRENTYEAR}} }}") using the [http://www.gnu.org/software/tar/manual/html_node/Date-input-formats.html GNU standard format]. * To remove protection currently applied to a page, modify the specific protection type (edit, move, creation) and set the protection level to "allow all users". Leave the settings unchanged on the protection types that you do not wish to modify. * Edit protection and move protection can be applied separately or together, and set to different protection levels and to expire after different lengths of timesds. ** If applying page protection to a completely unprotected page, the page protection form will automatically update the move protection settings to match the edit protection settings you enter, unless you specify different move protection settings. *** To specify move protection settings that are ''different'' than the edit protection settings you entered, tick the "unlock further protect options" checkbox and enter the move protection settings accordingly. *** To apply ''no'' move protection alongside edit protection, set the move protection level to "allow all users". * ''If you are not using an automated tool to modify or apply page protection, please update the [[Template:Protection templates|protection templates]] on the page after applying your changes.'' ;Important technical information * Avoid using non-integer values when entering an expiration (such as "2.37 weeks" or "1.84 days"). The MediaWiki software has interpreted non-integer expirations '''[[Wikipedia:Village stocks#SQL for the Time-Traveling Block Breaker award|very badly in the past]]''' before. * [[WP:PC1|Pending changes protection]] is unavailable for use on talk pages or anywhere within the user space. * Cascading protection is only available when applying full edit protection to a page. * The confirmed or autoconfirmed user right [[Special:ListGroupRights|is required]] in order for users to move ''any pages at all''; the page's protection status is completely irrelevant. ;Useful links * All pages that are currently protected from '''editing''' are publicly visible at [[Special:ProtectedPages]]. * All pages that are currently protected from '''creation (salted)''' are publicly visible at [[Special:ProtectedTitles]]. * All pages that are currently under '''pending changes protection''' are publicly visible at [[Special:StablePages]]. {{Collapse bottom}}
